reasoning: The unsafe verdict was based solely on a Docker script not used in the AUR build process. The PKGBUILD uses standard qmake/make without Docker, and all build/install hooks are safe.
disagreement_analysis: qwen incorrectly flagged src/fritzing-app/docker/build-linux.sh as unsafe due to unverified Docker image usage, but this script isn't executed during makepkg. deepseek correctly recognized it as irrelevant to AUR safety.
false_positives: qwen's unsafe flag on build-linux.sh: Overreacted to a CI script unused in AUR builds. Docker usage in unreferenced scripts isn't a threat.
learnings: Improve file prioritization: Models should deprioritize CI/docker scripts unless referenced in PKGBUILD/.install
Clarify scope in prompts: Emphasize that only files executed during makepkg (build/install hooks) are security-critical
Reduce false positives: Train models to distinguish between active build scripts vs. unused tooling in source trees
